_meta.description: Tactical 24h schedule the Phase-2 office simulation reads each simulated work day. agent-runner.js's runWorkDayCycle() iterates `blocks` in order within a single invocation (no per-block cron yet — see 'cron_status'). Each block represents a point in the simulated day where a specific activity fires, so the day's ~50-case CRM pool, meetings, AI-experience reports, and spare time are spread out instead of all happening at once.
_meta.cron_status: LIVE (re-enabled 2026-07-18 for the Q&A engine's first activation, after the 2026-07-08 pause). wrangler.toml has a single Cloudflare Cron Trigger ('*/30 0-13,23 * * *', every 30 min 23:00-23:30 + 00:00-13:30 UTC = 02:00-16:30 IDT), matching the owner's 02:00-16:30 Israel activity window. Each tick, agent-runner.js's scheduled() converts event.scheduledTime to Israel local time (israelTimeParts()) and calls runScheduledBlock(env, time, dayOfWeek), which is a no-op unless a block below has that exact `time` for that day-of-week (note: the 02:00-Israel blocks therefore fire at 23:00 UTC the PREVIOUS calendar day — israelTimeParts() handles the day-of-week rollover). Day-in-progress state lives in SIM_KV under 'daily-cycle-state' between blocks. Per-block errors (e.g. Gemini 429) are logged to `reports` (logScheduledError) and do not abort the day.

week_mapping.description: dayOfWeek = ((current_day - 1) % 7) + 1, as already computed by runWorkDayCycle(). This schedule maps it to an Israeli Sun-Thu work week per the Launch Decisions ('work days Sunday-Thursday'), with Friday as a short summary day and Saturday off.
week_mapping.6: Friday (short day — weekly summary meeting, reduced case load)
week_mapping.7: Saturday (off — idle only, no Gemini/Claude calls)

_meta.case_volume_design_note: SUPERSEDED 2026-07-18 (Q&A-engine rebuild) — volume is no longer a fixed per-agent quota at all. Once per day, at cycle start, agent-runner.js's computeDailyQuestionVolume() checks the remaining shared Claude budget for the month (config/token-economy.json shared_claude_budget) and picks a conservative day total from it (0 if exhausted) — never a fixed number decided in advance. That total is then SPREAD across the day exactly as before, via the existing case_batch case_share proportions below (6 case_batch blocks, 02:00-15:00 Israel, several hours apart; full_day_schedule has 11 blocks total — the 6 case batches plus tool_task_window, chore_rotation, report, standup meeting, and spare_time). Non-burstiness for Notebook-X specifically doesn't come from this per-day total at all — it comes from workers/gemini-pacer.js's per-call pacing check at ask time (agent-base.js askAssignedProject()), which is what actually rate-limits Gemini calls regardless of how many questions a block was handed.

overtime_window.note: Per-agent overtime eligibility comes from agents-config.json work_routine.overtime (e.g. agent_2 '30%_of_days', agent_5 'regular', agent_10 'daily'). Overtime cases are drawn from the same daily pool via extendSession(), not a separate pool.

blocks.label: Daily AI-experience reports + model-education case studies
Spare time: coworker interaction or idle (token-saving)

friday_schedule.label: Short day: reduced case load, weekly executive summary at 12:00

blocks.label: Daily AI-experience reports + model-education case studies
Weekly executive summary meeting + PDF/Excel generation
Spare time: coworker interaction or idle (token-saving)

saturday_schedule.label: Day off: no case batches, no meetings, no Gemini/Claude calls. Pure idle to preserve tokens.

model_education_program.description: Stress-tests Claude (data-center) and Gemini (Notebook-X) — the two production AI systems the office exists to evaluate. Runs once per work day at the 'report' block, after that day's question batches.

daily_ai_experience_report.who: every agent who asked at least one question today
daily_ai_experience_report.what: a short, casual fileStatusReport()-style note (1-2 paragraphs) on today's experience asking Claude/Gemini real questions: what worked, what didn't, tone matches the agent's personality.
daily_ai_experience_report.permission_level: private (staff/agents + owner) per CLAUDE.md report tiering — these are internal mood/experience notes, not business-facing.

capability_gap_reporting._meta: SUPERSEDED the old 'model_education_case_study' sub-block 2026-07-18 (Q&A-engine rebuild). No longer batched at end of day from 'the worst N interactions' — each ask-and-evaluate call (agents/agent-base.js askAssignedProject()) decides in real time via workers/gap-reports.js detectCapabilityGap() + the agent's own escalation_threshold (config/agents-config.json, see Step 3 persona differentiation).
capability_gap_reporting.trigger: HARD gaps (no Notebook-X answer at all, or the Claude request itself failed) always flag, any persona. SOFT candidates (a real but weak answer, quality < 0.5) only flag if quality is also below THIS agent's escalation_threshold — sensitive personas (QA, Lead QA) flag more of these than tolerant ones (Standard, Trainee).
capability_gap_reporting.what: a short (2-4 line) HEBREW internal office note, in the flagging agent's own voice via queryGemini(): what's missing/wrong, where (topic/notebook), what it teaches. Framed as "the tool I work with isn't good enough here, flagging it for the tool to be fixed" — internal office logic, not a customer-facing incident.
capability_gap_reporting.routing: agent.fileGapReport() persists a `reports` row (type='gap_hebrew', project set). Once per day at this 'report' block, agent-runner.js's fileGapDigests() reads today's rows, groups by project, and commits ONE file per project with entries to reports/gaps/<project>/<date>.md. NEVER a GitHub Issue, for either project — an explicit requirement of this rebuild, unlike the old model_education_case_study which did open one.
capability_gap_reporting.permission_level: special (AI staff + owner) — internal, Hebrew, not business-facing.

spare_time_program.description: Fires whenever an agent has no more assigned cases for the day and no meeting to attend. Two outcomes, chosen per agent:

coworker_interaction.what: one short Gemini call generating a brief in-character exchange between this agent and a related agent (relationships.json partnerships/rivalries preferred), logged as an `interactions` row (type='coworker_chat'). Costs exactly 1 Gemini call.

idle.what: agent goes idle: state is saved as-is, an `interactions` row (type='idle', no query/response) is logged for the daily summary, and ZERO Gemini/Claude calls are made. This is the default and MUST be the only path on Saturdays (force_idle).
idle.token_savings: this is the primary token-discipline lever for spare time, per Launch Decisions.

weekly_summary_program.description: Friday 12:00. Generates the week's executive package and bumps product versions for any asset_pipeline items that reached 'implemented' this week.

outputs.format: markdown, ~10 sections, print-ready (consistent with the existing index.html PDF Export convention — open and use the browser's Print to PDF for an actual 10-page PDF).
outputs.permission_level: private/special — internal weekly package, not for public distribution.

outputs.format: CSV (Excel-openable, no xlsx binary dependency per CLAUDE.md 'no build step, ever'). One row per agent: cases handled, mood start/end, irritation events, happy events, app-call count, comparisons, quality avg.
short markdown, business-facing excerpt (clean framing, no internal mood/score detail).

product_versioning.description: Each admin-tier agent (5-11, can_generate_assets: true) may own at most one 'weekly project' at a time, tracked as an agents/reports/asset-pipeline/board.json entry with a `product_id`. On the Friday weekly_summary_program, any board item whose stage advanced to 'implemented' this week bumps that product's version by +0.01 (starting at 1.00) in year_stats.stats.product_versions, and the board entry's `version` field is updated to match.
